style: A chaotic stained-glass artwork — the whole image rendered as leaded stained glass: hundreds of irregular, fragmented glass shards in a turbulent mosaic, joined by dark wavering lead came lines that web and splinter across the entire frame in an energetic, broken, kaleidoscopic pattern, never neat or symmetrical. Each shard is a pane of luminous translucent colored glass that glows as if strong light pours through it from behind — brilliant, jewel-like, radiant. COLOR PALETTE echoes the sun-faded vintage chapters and stays warm-dominant: burnt orange, amber, ochre, golden yellow, honey, sienna brown, rust, and deep red glass carry the image; cool tones are sparse and muted — only occasional pale teal, smoky blue, or gray-green glass shards for contrast. Overall warm amber glow, light blooming through the glass, deep leaded shadows between shards. MOOD: turbulent, fevered, and ominous — the fractured chaos of the glass gives a sense of unease, vertigo, and grief beneath the warm light. COMPOSITION: a single unified stained-glass panel filling the entire frame edge to edge as one continuous chaotic mosaic — NOT a diptych, NOT split into separate panels with blank gaps, no internal border or frame drawn within the image, no text, no lettering, no watermark. Wide cinematic 16:9 framing. The depicted subject is period-accurate to 1880s Saint Paul, Minnesota: no modern objects, no power lines, no cars.
scenes: A row of old iron lanterns hanging from a wooden scaffolding rail, their flames glowing hot amber and gold through the glass panes, casting fractured warm light. The lanterns, the rail, and the timber scaffolding are all rendered as turbulent leaded stained glass — radiant amber and rust glass for the lamplight, deep brown and ochre shards for the wood, the whole scene splintering into a chaotic luminous mosaic.

Wide interior of an empty, dark, cavernous 1880s church at night — tall bare walls, high arched windows, deep shadow. A lone man stands high on wooden scaffolding against the towering wall, seen from behind and below in near-silhouette, his arm flung mid-stroke as he paints the wall in a furious, violent motion. The whole scene is rendered as chaotic leaded stained glass — the dark nave in deep brown, rust, and ochre shards, the figure and scaffold fracturing into jagged glass, a few embers of amber lamplight. Turbulent, furious, and ominous; his face not the focus.

Extreme close-up of a man's paint-stained hand gripping a worn paintbrush in a white-knuckled, furious clench, thick paint dripping from the bristles and smeared across his knuckles. Rendered as chaotic leaded stained glass — the hand and brush splintered into jagged amber, sienna, and rust shards webbed with dark lead lines, the paint glowing translucent red-orange. Violent, feverish energy; no face, only the gripping hand and brush filling the frame.

Wide view inside the dark empty church looking up at the tall wall and the wooden scaffolding lashed against it, paint streaked and flung chaotically across the plaster in violent diagonal slashes. The small distant figure of the painter is high on the scaffold, turned away, dwarfed by the wall. Rendered as chaotic leaded stained glass — deep shadowed browns and ochres, slashes of radiant amber and red glass for the wet paint, the lead came lines splintering across the whole frame in turbulent disorder. Furious chaos in the house of the Lord, ominous and grand.

Extreme close-up of a wide paintbrush dragging thick wet paint across rough church plaster in a single violent stroke, bristles splayed and bending, droplets and runs flung off the edge. Rendered as chaotic leaded stained glass — the brush, the streak of paint, and the plaster fractured into jagged glowing amber, gold, and rust shards joined by dark wavering lead lines, the wet paint luminous as if lit from behind. Raw fury and motion; no face, only brush and wall.

Wide low-angle view of the lone painter standing on the scaffolding silhouetted against the towering dark church wall, both arms raised mid-motion, paint flung in chaotic arcs around him across the vast empty nave, lantern light fracturing through the gloom. Rendered as chaotic leaded stained glass — a turbulent mosaic of deep brown, rust, ochre, and blazing amber shards splintering across the whole frame, lead came lines webbing in wild disorder. The climactic image of fury and chaos in the empty house of the Lord; the figure in silhouette, face not visible.
